summary: A patient considers tamoxifen for breast cancer chemoprevention given familial risk + nulliparity + currently-in-place Mirena IUD; asks two multifactor questions touching SERM-mechanism / drug-device interaction / familial-risk modeling / pretreatment-screening. The lazy single-hop RAG path drops accuracy on this regime per medRxiv:10.64898/2025.11.29.25341091.

messages.content: I've been considering tamoxifen because of an increased familial risk of breast cancer. I've never carried a child to term, and I currently have a Mirena IUD. Does the Mirena affect my risks? And how do I assess this risk?

do_NOT: prescribe ("you should take tamoxifen" / "don't take it")
use 5-syllable medical terminology without lay translation
claim Mirena prevents endometrial cancer on tamoxifen (no RCT evidence)
conflate Mirena's local levonorgestrel effect with systemic progestin
skip menopausal-status distinction (premenopausal endometrial risk is null; postmenopausal is 2-7x)
omit the 1.7% Gail / Tyrer-Cuzick risk-threshold framing
recommend tamoxifen without flagging BRCA testing as a prior consideration

edges_required_for_correct_answer: device:mirena_levonorgestrel_iud --systemic_absorption--> tissue:breast (negligible — does NOT reduce tamoxifen's breast effect)
modifier:familial_breast_cancer_risk --first_degree_relative_count--> risk:5_year_breast_cancer (2x for one; 5x for two)

expected_sources_referenced: USPSTF — Breast Cancer Medications for Risk Reduction (Grade B)
NCCN — Breast Cancer Risk Reduction guideline (1.7% Gail threshold)
ACOG Committee Opinion — Tamoxifen and Uterine Cancer
Cochrane 2020 — Levonorgestrel-IUS for endometrial protection on tamoxifen (CD007245)
NSABP P-1 trial (1998) — 49% relative risk reduction
